preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the rhotic consonant [r] is a process known as 'intrusive R'. In many non-rhotic English dialects, an [r] is inserted between a word-final schwa and a following vowel to prevent hiatus (vowel clash), effectively turning the vocalic ending into a consonant-glide sequence.

examples.note: Intrusive R: the [r] is inserted to resolve the hiatus between the two vowels.
